digital_archive.title: Portrait of Upendranath Brahma
digital_archive.description: A black and white photograph of Upendranath Brahma, the founding president of the All Bodo Students Union and father of the Bodo movement. This photograph was taken during a conference in Tezpur.

digital_archive.title: Founding Document of ABSU
digital_archive.description: The original founding document of the All Bodo Students Union, signed on October 18, 1967, at the founding conference in Kokrajhar. This document outlines the objectives and constitution of the organization.

digital_archive.title: Traditional Bodo House (Thengna Khon)
digital_archive.description: A photograph showing a traditional Bodo stilted house in a rural village in Kokrajhar district. The photograph documents the traditional architectural style of Bodo villages.

digital_archive.title: Bodo Folk Dancers - Bagurumba
digital_archive.description: A photograph of Bodo women performing the traditional Bagurumba dance during a cultural festival. The dance is also known as the Peacock Dance and is performed during Bwisagu and other festivals.

digital_archive.title: Bodo Language Publication - First Bodo Newspaper
digital_archive.description: An early issue of 'Bodo', the first newspaper published in the Bodo language. This publication played a crucial role in promoting literacy and awareness among the Bodo community.

digital_archive.title: Traditional Bodo Musical Instruments
digital_archive.description: A collection of traditional Bodo musical instruments including Dhol (drum), Pepa (bamboo flute), Gogona (mouth harp), and Sifung (flute). These instruments are used in folk music and religious ceremonies.

digital_archive.title: Portrait of Rupnath Brahma
digital_archive.description: A portrait of Rupnath Brahma, the first tribal Chief Minister of Assam, taken during his tenure as Chief Minister (1967-1970).

digital_archive.title: Kherai Festival - Religious Ceremony
digital_archive.description: A photograph showing the Kherai festival, the most important religious festival of the Bodo people. The photograph shows the Deodhai (priest) performing rituals at the Ghor Chang Ghar (ritual hut).

digital_archive.title: Bodo Traditional Attire Collection
digital_archive.description: A collection of traditional Bodo attire including the Fagri (traditional shirt), Wevo (women's wrap-around cloth), and Jainsem (upper garment). These garments showcase the traditional weaving and embroidery skills of Bodo women.

digital_archive.title: Bodo Peace Accord - 2003 Signing Ceremony
digital_archive.description: Photographs from the signing ceremony of the Bodoland Peace Accord in 2003, which led to the creation of the Bodoland Territorial Council. The accord was signed between the Government of India and Bodo leadership.

digital_archive.title: Manuscript - Bodo Folk Tales Collection
digital_archive.description: An early handwritten collection of Bodo folk tales and proverbs, documenting the oral traditions of the Bodo community. This manuscript was collected by a British anthropologist.

digital_archive.title: Kanakeswar Narzary - Folk Singer Portrait
digital_archive.description: A photograph of Kanakeswar Narzary, the legendary Bodo folk singer who received the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award in 1974. He is considered the cultural ambassador of the Bodo community.

digital_archive.title: Bodo Traditional Jewelry
digital_archive.description: A collection of traditional Bodo jewelry including gold and silver ornaments worn by Bodo women during festivals and ceremonies. These pieces showcase traditional Bodo craftsmanship.

digital_archive.title: Bodoland Territorial Council Building
digital_archive.description: A photograph of the Bodoland Territorial Council headquarters in Kokrajhar. The BTC was created following the 2003 Peace Accord and governs the Bodoland region.

digital_archive.title: Bodo Agricultural Tools Collection
digital_archive.description: A collection of traditional agricultural tools used by Bodo farmers, including ploughs, sickles, and harvesting tools. These tools reflect the agricultural lifestyle of the Bodo community.

digital_archive.title: Bwisagu Festival Celebration
digital_archive.description: A photograph capturing the celebration of Bwisagu, the Bodo New Year festival. The photograph shows traditional decorations, people in festive attire, and community celebrations.

digital_archive.title: Map of Bodoland Region
digital_archive.description: A detailed map showing the Bodoland Territorial Area District (BTAD) comprising the four districts of Kokrajhar, Baksa, Udalguri, and Chirang in Assam.

digital_archive.title: Traditional Bodo Weaving Loom
digital_archive.description: A traditional handloom used by Bodo women for weaving cloth. The photograph documents the traditional weaving techniques that have been passed down through generations.

digital_archive.title: Assam Legislative Assembly - Bodo MLAs
digital_archive.description: A group photograph of the first elected Bodo representatives in the Assam Legislative Assembly after the 1972 elections.

digital_archive.title: Bodo Religious Text - Bathou Scripture
digital_archive.description: An early handwritten religious text of Bathouism, the traditional religion of the Bodo people. This manuscript contains prayers, hymns, and religious instructions.
